metadata.title: Centrifugal Pumps | Welltech®
metadata.description: Stainless steel centrifugal pumps for high-flow, continuous, and hygienic transfer of low-viscosity fluids like milk, juice, and water.

hero.title: Centrifugal Pumps
hero.desc: High flow and continuous transfer for low-viscosity fluids. Stainless design providing maximum hygiene for food and beverage lines.

technology.title: Centrifugal Technology
technology.desc: Centrifugal pumps operate on the principle of generating centrifugal force by rotating the impeller inside the casing at high speeds. The fluid is drawn from the center of the impeller and accelerated through the vanes to the discharge line. It is the most efficient and economical solution for transferring fluids such as milk, water, juice, and CIP washing liquids.

features.title: Design and Performance Advantages
items: Continuous and Smooth Flow at High Capacities
High Efficiency with Low Energy Consumption
100% Washable (CIP) Internal Structure with Zero Dead Legs
AISI 304 or 316L Stainless Steel Forged Casing
Open, Semi-Open, or Closed Impeller Options

table.title: Technical Specifications and Capacity
table.note: * Flow rate and head values are custom configured according to your process needs based on motor power and impeller diameter.

items.q: Can centrifugal pumps be used for highly viscous liquids?
items.a: No. Centrifugal pumps are designed for low-viscosity fluids like water, milk, and fruit juice. Highly viscous liquids like honey or cream will cause the impeller to cavitate.

items.q: What is the difference between open and closed impellers?
items.a: Closed impellers provide maximum efficiency in transferring pure liquids (water, milk), while open or semi-open impellers are used to transfer liquids containing light sediments or fruit particles without clogging.

cta.title: Accurate Flow and Pressure
cta.desc: Let us know the number of elbows and pipe length in your line, and we will determine the right impeller diameter that won't cause cavitation.
